title: Freehand: a page that is not alone
why.empty: Empty editor. Do not start from the code — start from the question "what do I want on this page". Then turn it into tags, one at a time.
why.no-nav: There is no navigation. A list of links is not a menu until you say it is a menu. There is a tag for exactly that, and it exists so the browser can tell it apart from every other list.
why.no-img: There is no image. The path is /uroci/bike.jpg. It has nothing to do with your topic and that is fine — what matters is the tag, not the bike.
why.no-alt: The image is there, but it is mute. If it does not load — and for some people, some day, it will not — there will be a hole where it stood. Say what is on it.
why.no-link: There is not a single link. Your page sits alone on the internet, and the internet is precisely this: pages that lead to one another.
why.no-outside: Your links only lead back into you. The task asks for at least one going out — to something genuinely worth reading on your topic. The address starts with http.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
blocks.0.text: Empty editor. You know what comes next.
blocks.2.text: Your topic again. But this time the page does not stand alone — it leads somewhere and it shows something.
blocks.3.text: It must have: a navigation menu, the kind a real site would have. An image that says what is on it, even when it is missing. At least one link out — to something worth reading on your topic. And text that holds it all together.
blocks.4.text: The image is /uroci/bike.jpg or /uroci/bike2.jpg. They have nothing to do with your topic and that is fine — what matters is the tag, not the bike.
blocks.5.text: I am not giving you a skeleton. I am not telling you which tag goes where. You decide.
blocks.6.text: If you freeze at the blank screen
blocks.7.text: That is normal. Do not start from the code — start from the question "what do I want on this page". Write it out in plain words, as a list on paper. Then turn it into tags, one at a time.
blocks.8.text: A blank file is not a memory test. It is a test of whether you can decide what you want.
blocks.10.text: The menu will be a stacked, bulleted list. The image will be too big. Everything will be serif. You know this by now: you did not do it wrong, there is simply no CSS yet.
blocks.11.text: But look at what you have. Three sections ago you typed "Hello" and wondered what happened. Now you are building a page with navigation, media and links out into the world. With nobody helping, from an empty file.
